seoTitle: Tichborne vs Tighes Hill — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Tichborne and Tighes Hill. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Tichborne vs Tighes Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Tichborne and Tighes Hill.
heading: Tichborne vs Tighes Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tichborne, New South Wales and Tighes Hill, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tichborne has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Tighes Hill (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tichborne or Tighes Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tichborne has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Tighes Hill scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tichborne scores 59/100 for bushfire risk. Tighes Hill scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tichborne or Tighes Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tichborne has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
